members.title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk
Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk
Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports
Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports

strategies.label: Secure remote admin access (guided choices)

fields.description: How would you like to secure remote access?

options.label: Add restricted access without removing old public rules
options.impact_text: Public SSH/RDP access on ports 22 and 3389 will remain until you manually remove the 0.0.0.0/0 rules. New restricted rules will be added.

options.label: Close public and auto-remove old rules
options.impact_text: All existing 0.0.0.0/0 rules on ports 22 and 3389 will be automatically removed. Make sure you have alternative access (SSM, VPN) before applying.

options.label: Restrict to my IP
options.impact_text: Only traffic from the provided CIDR will be allowed on ports 22 and 3389. All other sources will be denied.

options.label: Restrict to custom CIDR
options.impact_text: Only traffic from the provided CIDR range (for example, office/VPN) will be allowed on ports 22 and 3389.

fields.description: Allowed IPv4 CIDR for SSH/RDP (for example, office or VPN range).
Optional allowed IPv6 CIDR for SSH/RDP.

dependency_checks.message: No specialized dependency checks are available for this strategy yet.

warnings: Restricting or revoking public admin ingress can lock out operators. Confirm SSM, VPN, or bastion access before apply.
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver used the compatibility-safe default 'close_public' for strategy 'sg_restrict_public_ports_guided'.

members.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public access
S3 general purpose buckets should block public access
S3 general purpose buckets should block public write access

strategies.label: Block public access on bucket

dependency_checks.message: Runtime probes could not fully determine bucket public-access posture. Validate direct bucket access dependencies before applying this strategy. Analyze the affected S3 bucket policy/ACL/public-access-block settings, the bucket KMS key policy/grants (if SSE-KMS), CloudFront OAC/OAI configuration, and any VPC endpoint or cross-account IAM principals that access the bucket. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, this validation can be automated.
Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).

warnings: May break workloads that depend on direct public S3 access. Analyze the affected S3 bucket policy/ACL/public-access-block settings, the bucket KMS key policy/grants (if SSE-KMS), CloudFront OAC/OAI configuration, and any VPC endpoint or cross-account IAM principals that access the bucket. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, dependency validation can be automated.
strategies.impact_text: All four bucket-level public access block settings will be enabled for this bucket.
blocked_reasons: Runtime evidence could not prove the bucket is private and website hosting is disabled.
Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver downgraded strategy 's3_bucket_block_public_access_standard' to manual S3.2 preservation profile 's3_bucket_block_public_access_manual_preservation'. Runtime evidence could not prove the bucket is private and website hosting is disabled. Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).

strategies.label: Migrate to CloudFront + OAC + private S3

dependency_checks.message: Runtime probes could not fully determine bucket public-access posture. Validate direct bucket access dependencies before applying this strategy. Analyze the affected S3 bucket policy/ACL/public-access-block settings, the bucket KMS key policy/grants (if SSE-KMS), CloudFront OAC/OAI configuration, and any VPC endpoint or cross-account IAM principals that access the bucket. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, this validation can be automated.
Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).

warnings: Requires careful validation of bucket policies and KMS permissions. Analyze the affected S3 bucket policy/ACL/public-access-block settings, the bucket KMS key policy/grants (if SSE-KMS), CloudFront OAC/OAI configuration, and any VPC endpoint or cross-account IAM principals that access the bucket. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, dependency validation can be automated.
strategies.impact_text: Bucket-level public access block settings will be enabled and access will be served through CloudFront + OAC.
blocked_reasons: Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver downgraded strategy 's3_migrate_cloudfront_oac_private' to manual S3.2 preservation profile 's3_migrate_cloudfront_oac_private_manual_preservation'. Unable to inspect bucket website configuration (AccessDenied).

members.title: S3 general purpose buckets should require requests to use SSL

strategies.label: Enforce SSL-only S3 requests (strict deny)

fields.description: Merge with existing bucket policy statements instead of replacing.
fields.impact_text: After applying, non-HTTPS requests to this bucket will be denied (HTTP requests receive 403).

dependency_checks.message: Non-TLS clients and legacy integrations will fail after strict SSL policy enforcement.
Strict SSL enforcement can conflict with existing bucket policy statements.

warnings: Non-TLS clients or legacy integrations will fail after apply.

If the bucket already has a policy, capture it before apply with `aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket <BUCKET_NAME> --query Policy --output text > pre-remediation-policy.json`; rollback restores it with `aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket <BUCKET_NAME> --policy file://pre-remediation-policy.json`.
strategies.impact_text: After applying, all HTTP (non-HTTPS) requests to this bucket will receive a 403 Forbidden response.
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver kept S3.5 strategy 's3_enforce_ssl_strict_deny' executable because merge-safe policy preservation evidence is available.

strategies.label: Enforce SSL with principal exemptions

fields.description: Merge with existing bucket policy statements instead of replacing.
fields.impact_text: After applying, non-HTTPS requests to this bucket will be denied (HTTP requests receive 403).

fields.description: IAM principal ARNs exempted from the strict deny rule.

dependency_checks.message: Review principal exemptions carefully to avoid over-broad bypass paths.

warnings: Exemptions weaken blanket enforcement and must be tightly scoped.

If the bucket already has a policy, capture it before apply with `aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket <BUCKET_NAME> --query Policy --output text > pre-remediation-policy.json`; rollback restores it with `aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket <BUCKET_NAME> --policy file://pre-remediation-policy.json`.
strategies.impact_text: After applying, all HTTP (non-HTTPS) requests to this bucket will receive a 403 Forbidden response.
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver kept S3.5 strategy 's3_enforce_ssl_with_principal_exemptions' executable because merge-safe policy preservation evidence is available.

members.title: IAM root user access key should not exist

strategies.label: Disable root access key

fields.description: How would you like to handle the active root access key?

options.label: Disable key (recommended)
options.impact_text: The root access key will be set to Inactive. You can re-enable it later if needed. This is the safest first step.

options.label: Delete key permanently
options.impact_text: The root access key will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone. Root MFA must be active.

dependency_checks.message: Confirm break-glass and emergency automation does not depend on root access keys.

warnings: Root credentials required. This remediation is manual/high-risk and must follow the runbook. Runbook: docs/prod-readiness/root-credentials-required-iam-root-access-key-absent.md.
Disabling root keys can impact break-glass automations that still use root credentials.
blocked_reasons: IAM.4 execution is handled exclusively by /api/root-key-remediation-runs.
strategies.decision_rationale: IAM.4 strategy 'iam_root_key_disable' is exposed here as metadata only. Use /api/root-key-remediation-runs for root-key lifecycle execution.

strategies.label: Delete root access key

fields.description: How would you like to handle the active root access key?

options.label: Disable key (recommended)
options.impact_text: The root access key will be set to Inactive. You can re-enable it later if needed. This is the safest first step.

options.label: Delete key permanently
options.impact_text: The root access key will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone. Root MFA must be active.

dependency_checks.message: Confirm break-glass and emergency automation does not depend on root access keys.
Root key deletion is irreversible; ensure fallback access is validated.
Root MFA enrollment is active (AccountMFAEnabled=1).

warnings: Root credentials required. This remediation is manual/high-risk and must follow the runbook. Runbook: docs/prod-readiness/root-credentials-required-iam-root-access-key-absent.md.
Gate: Root MFA must be active before this delete path is selectable. If AccountMFAEnabled=0, strategy selection is blocked.
Deleting root keys is irreversible and requires validated fallback access.
blocked_reasons: IAM.4 execution is handled exclusively by /api/root-key-remediation-runs.
strategies.decision_rationale: IAM.4 strategy 'iam_root_key_delete' is exposed here as metadata only. Use /api/root-key-remediation-runs for root-key lifecycle execution.

members.title: IAM root user access key should not exist

strategies.label: Disable root access key

fields.description: How would you like to handle the active root access key?

options.label: Disable key (recommended)
options.impact_text: The root access key will be set to Inactive. You can re-enable it later if needed. This is the safest first step.

options.label: Delete key permanently
options.impact_text: The root access key will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone. Root MFA must be active.

dependency_checks.message: Confirm break-glass and emergency automation does not depend on root access keys.

warnings: Root credentials required. This remediation is manual/high-risk and must follow the runbook. Runbook: docs/prod-readiness/root-credentials-required-iam-root-access-key-absent.md.
Disabling root keys can impact break-glass automations that still use root credentials.
blocked_reasons: IAM.4 execution is handled exclusively by /api/root-key-remediation-runs.
strategies.decision_rationale: IAM.4 strategy 'iam_root_key_disable' is exposed here as metadata only. Use /api/root-key-remediation-runs for root-key lifecycle execution.

strategies.label: Delete root access key

fields.description: How would you like to handle the active root access key?

options.label: Disable key (recommended)
options.impact_text: The root access key will be set to Inactive. You can re-enable it later if needed. This is the safest first step.

options.label: Delete key permanently
options.impact_text: The root access key will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone. Root MFA must be active.

dependency_checks.message: Confirm break-glass and emergency automation does not depend on root access keys.
Root key deletion is irreversible; ensure fallback access is validated.
Root MFA enrollment is active (AccountMFAEnabled=1).

warnings: Root credentials required. This remediation is manual/high-risk and must follow the runbook. Runbook: docs/prod-readiness/root-credentials-required-iam-root-access-key-absent.md.
Gate: Root MFA must be active before this delete path is selectable. If AccountMFAEnabled=0, strategy selection is blocked.
Deleting root keys is irreversible and requires validated fallback access.
blocked_reasons: IAM.4 execution is handled exclusively by /api/root-key-remediation-runs.
strategies.decision_rationale: IAM.4 strategy 'iam_root_key_delete' is exposed here as metadata only. Use /api/root-key-remediation-runs for root-key lifecycle execution.
